arts.ttl: I played my best football at Celtic - the supporters are greatest in the world
arts.sum: Mark Viduka insists Celtic have the greatest fans in the world.

arts.ttl: Forgotten Celtic ace sees transfer value soar to £8m as he scoops end-of-year gong
arts.sum: Celtic loan star Alexandro Bernabei has been named in the Brazilian FA's team of the season.

arts.ttl: Celtic issue urgent warning after supporters 'targeted in Zagreb'
arts.sum: Celtic club chiefs have issued an urgent warning to supporters after fans were targeted in Zagreb.

arts.ttl: I managed Celtic in proper Champions League - new format is rubbish
arts.sum: Gordon Strachan has revealed he is not a fan of the revamped league phase of the Champions League - even if he reckons it will help Celtic advance.

arts.ttl: Auston Trusty talks Celtic chances of Champions League progression as he savours every moment
arts.sum: Speaking ahead of Celtic’s trip to face Dinamo Zagreb, Trusty has made it clear that he is savouring every moment of being present at this level and shared why the Bhoys are mentally prepared to see qualification over the line. Auston Trusty has been able to fulfil a lifelong dream of competing in the Champions League after his move to Celtic in the summer.

arts.ttl: I'm the new Luka Modric wanted by two of Premier League's biggest clubs, but first I'm facing Celtic in Champions League
arts.sum: Martin Baturina has been compared to Luka Modric There are literally countless others who have squared-off against the Hoops including Real Madrid heavyweight Luka Modric for Los Blancos not too long ago.

arts.ttl: Alexandro Bernabei Celtic transfer latest as Luke McCowan prediction made – Pakhead state of play
arts.sum: Bernabei has been rewarded for his stellar displays with Brazilian top-flight club Internacional

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers pinpoints reason why Celtic hold belief that they can claim Dinamo Zagreb result
arts.sum: Celtic boss Brendan Rodgers has taken his side from the bottom of their Champions League group last year to within one victory of securing progression to the knockout playoff phase. Enjoying an upward trajectory, Celtic have taken two wins and two draws from their opening five Champions League matches this campaign, with a victory against Dinamo Zagreb tonight set to be enough to reach the next stage.

arts.ttl: Dinamo Zagreb vs Celtic preview: Rodgers confident of ending CL away win drought
arts.sum: “To win away from home, because you know what it means to supporters as well as the points and you know that an away win at any level, but in particular in the Champions League, is a difficult challenge, it’s one that gives a real good feeling,” Rodgers said at his pre-match media briefing in the Maksimir Stadium on Monday night. Asked if he felt his team were now operating at a level where they should be confident about winning away in the Champions League, Rodgers said: “I don’t think there’s any doubt.

arts.ttl: Peter Lawwell praises club for keeping Celtic fans ‘silent’ – Brendan Rodgers’ side ‘very happy’ with outcome
arts.sum: Celtic will take on Dinamo Zagreb in the Champions League today after Brendan Rodgers’ side settled for a 1-1 draw against Club Brugge in their last European tie. Cameron Carter-Vickers scored an own goal, and Daizen Maeda netted the equaliser in at Celtic Park against Club Brugge.
